LENS:

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# BRIEF V4 — THE MISSION IS TO **BEAT** 0.5578. ONE SINGLE PLAN. UNLEASH EVERYTHING.
2	
3	## 0. THE OPERATOR'S DIRECTION (this is the frame; do not narrow it)
4	> "Why stick with bodhi class? Why not the top one? ... If we have any old rules that hurt us from score, just
5	> drop it. If we don't have enough corpus to make us SOTA, then kick on the agentic outline search, or we do
6	> better at the early query generation + search + fetch + select + weight + dedup + consolidate, make them
7	> much much much better and richer so we are SOTA. Don't stuck with anything. I need one single solid plan,
8	> unleash the full power at the beginning, then keep the hamster wheel running till we match SOTA and beat SOTA."
9	
10	**TARGET: > 0.5578 (cellcog-max, #1). NOT parity. NOT bodhi. THE TOP.**
11	Every previous plan (mine, Fable's, Sol's) quietly lowered the bar to match what had already been built.
12	**That is the failure mode to avoid. Design for #1.**
13	
14	## 1. THE BOARD (task 72, our evaluator = GPT-5.5, from the benchmark's own per-task raw_results)
15	| system | OVERALL | compreh | INSIGHT | instr-follow | readability |
16	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
17	| **cellcog-max** | **0.5578** (corpus-wide #1; no per-task breakdown published — its task-72 score may be HIGHER than bodhi's) | 0.5634 | 0.5708 | 0.5530 | 0.5194 |
18	| bodhi (best *known* task-72 score) | 0.5441 | 0.5441 | 0.5457 | 0.5559 | 0.5199 |
19	| lunon | 0.5406 | | 0.5594 | | |
20	| WhaleCloud | 0.5396 | | | | |
21	| gemini-2.5-pro-DR = **THE REFERENCE** (parity=0.50) | 0.5102 | 0.5111 | 0.5035 | 0.5187 | 0.5100 |
22	| openai-DR | 0.4743 | | | | |
23	| **POLARIS** | **0.4382** | 0.4549 | 0.4238 | 0.4409 | **0.3774** (worst on the entire board) |
24	Weights: **INSIGHT 0.32 | COMPREHENSIVENESS 0.29 | INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING 0.25 | READABILITY 0.14.**
25	Measurement: judge SD **0.0074**; k=5 paired => **smallest resolvable effect +0.0094**. NEVER score n=1 again.
26	
27	## 2. THE THREE CAUSES OF THE GAP (all measured, none disputed)
28	**A. THE JUDGE CANNOT SEE A SINGLE ONE OF OUR SOURCES.**
29	RACE runs an LLM cleaner that deletes every `[n]` marker and the entire reference list BEFORE judging.
30	MEASURED on 12 real facts x 5 citation formats through the PRODUCTION cleaner:
31	| format | authors survive | journals |
32	|---|---|---|
33	| `[1]` markers (**WHAT WE DO — all 240 of them**) | **0/12** | 0/12 |
34	| `(Acemoglu & Restrepo, 2019)` — the "obvious" fix | **0/12** | 0/12 |
35	| `Acemoglu and Restrepo (2019) show...` | 5/12 | 0/12 |
36	| **`Writing in the JEP in 2019, Acemoglu and Restrepo show...`** | **10/12** | **12/12** |
37	**It is the JOURNAL NAME that anchors the clause as prose. And the year MUST be prose — every one of
38	cellcog's 281 parenthetical `(YYYY)` is deleted.** What the judge reads from us today:
39	*"About 47 percent of total US employment is at risk of computerisation."* — a naked, unattributed
40	assertion. That is Frey & Osborne, 5,223 citations, peer-reviewed. **We did the scholarly work and hid
41	every trace of it.** On a task whose instruction is *"only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles."*
42	
43	**B. WE CANNOT REASON ACROSS SOURCES — BY CONSTRUCTION.**
44	`entailment_judge.py:588` NEUTRAL clause deletes any sentence introducing *"a fact, entity, MECHANISM ... NOT
45	present in the SPAN"*. A cross-source inference IS that. **98% of our sentences are bare facts; bodhi's are ~45%
46	analysis; cellcog's synthesis prose is most of the document.** INSIGHT is 0.32 — the heaviest weight — and our worst.
47	
48	**C. OUR REPORT IS PHYSICALLY UNREADABLE.**
49	12 paragraphs, **median 677 words**, ZERO H3. Every other system on the board — *including the two we beat* —
50	sits at 38-170 words/paragraph. **Our readability (0.3774) is the WORST SCORE ON THE BOARD.** And the judge scores
51	all ~25 criteria in ONE call, writing a comparative analysis BEFORE the numbers => **cross-dimension bleed is real:
52	our wall-of-text impression taxes the other 86% of the score.**
53	
54	## 3. **THE FAITHFULNESS LINE — CORRECTED. THIS IS THE MOST IMPORTANT SECTION.**
55	I previously told the operator there was a hard trade-off: *"bodhi wins by writing sentences no single source
56	supports; we cannot match that without fabricating."* **THAT WAS WRONG.** Look at what bodhi actually writes:
57	
58	> *"...aggregate impacts were too small to detect over the period studied **[3]** — a pattern consistent with GPT
59	> diffusion lags and within-firm reorganization preceding macro reallocation **[1]**."*
60	
61	**IT CITES TWO SOURCES.** [3] is the empirical null. [1] is the GPT/productivity-paradox paper **WHICH STATES THE
62	DIFFUSION-LAG MECHANISM**. **bodhi is not inventing a mechanism. It is citing a THEORY paper for the mechanism and
63	an EMPIRICAL paper for the finding, and connecting them. THAT IS NOT HALLUCINATION — THAT IS WHAT A LITERATURE
64	REVIEW IS.**
65	
66	Our gate kills that sentence for a stupid reason: it takes the UNION of the cited spans and asks *"is every
67	assertion literally present?"* The composite claim is verbatim in neither — **even though BOTH ITS COMPONENTS ARE
68	FULLY GROUNDED IN THE TWO PAPERS IT CITES.**
69	
70	**THE CORRECTED LINE — design to this:**
71	- **FABRICATING A FACT** — a number, a study, a finding, an attribution that does not exist. **FRAUD. NEVER.**
72	- **CONNECTING GROUNDED CLAIMS** — a finding from paper A + a mechanism stated by paper B, with BOTH CITED.
73	  **SCHOLARSHIP. ALWAYS ALLOWED.**
74	**A mechanism may be asserted if ANY source in the corpus states it AND we cite that source.** (The contract at
75	`scripts/synthesis_contract.py` currently requires the mechanism to be stated by the two papers being compared —
76	**too strict, it forbids exactly bodhi's winning move. FIX IT.**)
77	**STILL DELETED, DETERMINISTICALLY:** any number not in a source | any study/organisation that does not exist |
78	any attribution to someone who did not say it | any mechanism NO paper anywhere states | forecasts | universals.
79	
80	**DROP ANY OTHER RULE THAT COSTS US SCORE AND BUYS US NOTHING.** The operator's instruction is explicit. Audit the
81	pipeline for rules that are pure cost: the 15-sources-per-section rule (unsatisfiable, silently padded), the
82	tier gate, the `[n]` marker convention itself (invisible to the judge — the bibliography can STAY for provenance,
83	but the PROSE must carry the attribution).
84	
85	## 4. THE CORPUS IS THE OTHER HALF — AND OUR RETRIEVAL IS AIMED WRONG
86	MEASURED: `data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json` = **997 evidence rows, 919 distinct URLs, 530 domains,
87	206 DOIs, 107 journals — and EVERY row already carries a direct quote.** But:
88	- **only 5 rows had AUTHOR NAMES** (which is why in-prose attribution was impossible);
89	- after Crossref enrichment, **120 distinct journal articles — of which only 17 ARE ON-TOPIC.** The rest is
90	  **ResNet, the BMJ PRISMA checklist, Cognitive Psychology 1974 (reading automaticity), Journal of Finance 1996.**
91	  **Prestigious, peer-reviewed, and completely irrelevant.**
92	- Top domains retrieved: researchgate.net (57), bls.gov (29), oecd.org (20), ssrn (12), nber (12) — **grey literature.**
93	**WE DID NOT HAVE A CORPUS PROBLEM. WE HAD AN AIMING PROBLEM.**
94	
95	**cellcog has ~98 ON-TOPIC canonical journal articles. We have 17 (+32 I hand-built tonight by citation-graph
96	expansion) = ~45. THAT IS NOT ENOUGH AND WE MUST NOT SETTLE FOR IT.**
97	
98	**WE HAVE AN AGENTIC RETRIEVAL LOOP WE NEVER USED FOR THIS.** (`PG_AGENTIC_*`: max 15 rounds, 120 queries,
99	convergence on theme saturation + URL overlap; `outline_agent`; the whole query-gen -> search -> fetch -> select ->
100	weight -> dedup -> consolidate chain.) **I spent hours doing MANUAL API pulls while the system's own search agent
101	sat idle. Fix the retrieval AT THE ROOT and run it to SATURATION against a COVERAGE MATRIX, not to a number I picked.**
102	
103	PROVEN TONIGHT: **keyword search cannot find this literature.** Autor-Levy-Murnane (2003, QJE, 4,743 cites) is the
104	most important paper in the field and its title — *"The Skill Content of Recent Technological Change"* — contains
105	**neither "AI" nor "labor market"**. No keyword query finds it. **CITATION-GRAPH EXPANSION does.** Build that in.
106	
107	## 5. WHAT #1 LOOKS LIKE (measured from cellcog's own task-72 artifact)
108	| | cellcog (#1) | POLARIS today |
109	|---|---|---|
110	| on-topic journal sources | ~98 | 22 |
111	| body words | 13,580 | 7,742 |
112	| structure | 9 H2 + **31 H3 + 8 H4** | 11 H2, **0 H3** |
113	| median paragraph | ~100w | **677w** |
114	| in-prose attributions | **133 author-year + 65 journal names** | **~0 visible** |
115	| epistemic labels | **14** (`[Established finding]`) — **THE ONLY SYSTEM ON THE BOARD THAT DOES THIS** | none |
116	| structured abstract | Objective / Methods / Findings / Contributions — **survives the cleaner in full** | none |
117	| **"Scope, Methods, and Source Selection" section** | **narrates its journal-only criteria IN PROSE — survives the cleaner 100% — it EXPLAINS ITS COMPLIANCE TO THE GRADER**, even pre-empting the working-paper objection | **we say nothing** |
118	| analysis vs reporting | ~half analysis | **2%** |
119	| `[n]` markers | 0 | 240 |
120	**"4IR" secretly means "COMPARE AI TO THE PREVIOUS THREE INDUSTRIAL REVOLUTIONS"** — ~11.5% of the score across
121	three criteria. We drop it after paragraph one.
122	**Our report OPENS with meta-commentary about itself** (*"This report synthesizes the retrieved research evidence
123	on the question above"* — there is no question above) — **and the judge reads the opening and closing most carefully.**
124	
125	## 6. **HOW WE BEAT cellcog, NOT JUST MATCH IT**
126	cellcog's claims are ASSERTED. **OURS CAN BE VERIFIED — every sentence traceable to a span in a real paper.**
127	The rubric explicitly grades **"Data and Factual Support: provides sufficient data, facts, cases, or evidence to
128	support its arguments and analysis"** (comprehensiveness) and **"Depth and Representativeness of Literature
129	Synthesized"**. **That is a criterion we can OUT-SCORE cellcog on, not merely match.**
130	**THE PLAY: cellcog's document architecture + cellcog's adjudication + DEEPER EVIDENCE THAN cellcog + grounding
131	cellcog does not have.** That is a #1 play. Copying bodhi never was.
132	
133	## 7. WHAT IS ALREADY BUILT (reuse it; fix what is wrong)
134	- `scripts/corpus_audit.py` / `enrich_bibliography.py` / `corpus_enrich_merge.py` — Crossref enrichment (works).
135	- `scripts/journal_corpus_build.py` — citation-graph expansion from canonical anchors (works; **this is how you
136	  find the real literature**).
137	- `scripts/journal_corpus_fetch.py` + `deep_fetch.py` — Crossref abstracts + Unpaywall OA full text.
138	- `scripts/cleaner_survival_test.py` — **proved which citation format survives. Reuse the finding.**
139	- `scripts/synthesis_contract.py` — typed adjudication, 14/14, **ZERO false admissions. But its mechanism rule is
140	  TOO STRICT (see §3) — fix it to allow a mechanism stated by ANY cited source.**
141	- `scripts/cellcog_composer.py` — first cut of the composer (evidence cards -> attributed, adjudicated prose).
142	- Baseline pinned: rank10 = **0.4382** (k=5, SD 0.0074).
143	
144	## 8. YOUR TASK — ONE SINGLE, COMPLETE, EXECUTABLE PLAN TO **BEAT 0.5578**
145	Cover, end to end:
146	1. **RETRIEVAL AT THE ROOT** — query generation, search, fetch, select, weight, dedup, consolidate. Journal-first.
147	   Citation-graph expansion. A COVERAGE MATRIX as the stopping condition (every restructuring dimension and every
148	   industry the task names). How many papers, and how do we know when we have enough?
149	2. **THE EVIDENCE LAYER** — cards with verbatim spans + declared fields (level/horizon/method/mechanism), canonical
150	   dedup, quality weighting.
151	3. **THE SYNTHESIS LANE** — the corrected faithfulness line. Typed adjudication. What EXACTLY may a sentence assert?
152	4. **THE COMPOSER** — cellcog's architecture: H2/H3/H4, ~100w paragraphs, in-prose journal-named attribution,
153	   epistemic labels, structured abstract, Scope-and-Methods section, 4IR as an organising spine, industry coverage.
154	5. **WHAT OLD RULES TO DROP** — name them, with the evidence that they cost score and buy nothing.
155	6. **THE WHEEL** — fix -> compose -> score k=5 -> read the prose line by line -> fix again. What is the loop, and
156	   what is the stopping condition?
157	7. **HONEST CEILING + KILL RULES.** If your plan does not beat 0.5578, say so and name exactly what is missing.

1	# Verdict
2	
3	POLARIS cannot reach the cellcog profile by rewriting the banked report. It needs:
4	
5	1. A journal-only, canonical evidence corpus.
6	2. Source-level evidence cards rather than isolated spans.
7	3. A dual-lane composer that separates factual claims from premise-linked synthesis.
8	4. Narrative author/journal attribution that survives cleaning.
9	5. An outline-first renderer producing roughly 34 claim-first H3s and 12–15k cleaned body words.
10	
11	My estimated outcome for the complete redesign is **0.53–0.56**, centered near **0.55**. Bodhi parity is credible; 0.5578 is an aggressive but defensible target. Attribution alone will not get close.
12	
13	## Corrections to the brief
14	
15	Several premises do not survive inspection.
16	
17	- **POLARIS does not currently cite 97 journal articles.** It has 105 numbered bibliography rows and approximately 97 canonical sources after duplicates, but many are working papers, institutional reports, news articles, blogs, vendor pages, and mirrors. Examples include WEF, MIT Sloan, NBER, IZA, arXiv, OECD, Harvard Gazette, Substack, Toptal, IMF working papers, Goldman Sachs, vendor statistics, and J.P. Morgan. The report itself says only 6% of its material is T1 primary research. Retrieval depth is therefore not yet journal-literature depth.
18	
19	- **The cheap attribution transform is conditional.** Narratively attributing the current corpus would expose sources such as WEF, Goldman Sachs, Toptal, and Substack more clearly, potentially lowering “Exclusive Citation of High-Quality Journal Articles.” Corpus filtering must precede—or at least gate—attribution.
20	
21	- **RACE cleaning is model-driven, not deterministic.** The cleaner sends each article or chunk to an LLM with instructions to remove citation marks, links, bibliographies, and footnotes. The observed local result may indeed contain zero markers, but that is an empirical output, not a regex guarantee. [The cleaner prompt says exactly this](https://github.com/Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench/blob/main/prompt/clean_prompt.py).
22	
23	- **“The judge sees no sources at all” is too strong.** It loses the numbered markers and reference list, but retains existing narrative references. POLARIS already names Acemoglu, Restrepo, Autor, Frey, Osborne, Felten, Raj, Seamans, Brynjolfsson, and several others. It also names at least *Journal of Economic Perspectives*, *Strategic Management Journal*, and *Science*. The sourcing signal is very weak, not literally zero.
24	
25	- **The table’s “10 authors / 1 journal” labels are not reproducible literally.** The raw POLARIS text contains more than twenty distinct author surnames and at least three journal venues. Those numbers may come from a narrower attribution-pattern regex, but then the metric needs that definition.
26	
27	- **The marker counts are reconcilable:** approximately 240 body occurrences plus 105 bibliography labels gives the reported 345 items entering cleaning.
28	
29	- **“No credit for being a better 7” is false as written.** Scores are continuous. A 7.8 beats a 7.0. Crossing 8 may be especially salient to the judge, but it is not a hard threshold. The bands are guidance, not quantization. [The scoring prompt explicitly uses continuous 0–10 scores](https://github.com/Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench/blob/main/prompt/score_prompt_en.py).
30	
31	- **Cellcog’s synthesis is not entirely fact-free.** “The task-based framework subsumes…” and “the earlier frameworks did not anticipate…” are theoretical assertions. They can be faithful when derived from already grounded premises, but they are more than stylistic glue. The safe target is not “unverified prose with no numbers”; it is **typed, premise-linked adjudication**.
32	
33	- Cellcog has exactly **31 H3 headings** by direct heading enumeration. POLARIS has zero. Cellcog’s bibliography contains about 104 rows; “98 distinct sources” appears to mean distinct body-cited works, not bibliography entries.
34	
35	The grader facts do check out: task 72 has 25 criteria—6 comprehensiveness, 5 insight, 7 instruction-following, and 7 readability—with weights 0.29/0.32/0.25/0.14. The complete task-specific rubric is in [criteria.jsonl](https://github.com/Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench/blob/main/data/criteria_data/criteria.jsonl). Both articles and all criteria are presented to one comparative scoring call, and the final calculation is target/(target+reference), as implemented in [deepresearch_bench_race.py](https://github.com/Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench/blob/main/deepresearch_bench_race.py).

39	```text
40	retrieval

321	
322	- Worker, firm, platform, regional, or national level.
323	- Experimental, quasi-experimental, observational, qualitative, or meta-analytic design.
324	- Exposure versus realized outcome.
325	- Short versus longer horizon.
326	- Employment, wages, productivity, labor share, skills, or autonomy.
327	- Institutional setting and industry.
328	
329	This is how POLARIS can explain apparent contradictions without inventing a mechanism. For example, firm growth and worker losses are not automatically contradictory when they use different observational levels. The safe analytical statement is about estimands and scope; a causal institutional explanation needs a grounded source.
330	
331	| Rubric moved | Expected raw movement |
332	|---|---:|
333	| “Balanced Discussion of AI’s Labor Market Impacts” | +0.5 to +1.0 |

356	   - Zero unsupported admissions is a release requirement.
357	
358	4. **Outline and two-module micro-compose**
359	   - One theory module and one cross-industry contradiction module.
360	   - Inspect the sentence ledger, not just the prose.
361	
362	5. **Full compose**
363	   - Run three independent compositions.
364	   - Score each artifact five times.
365	   - With generation SD ≈0.016, three composes reduce generation SE to about 0.0092; five judges reduce the per-artifact judge SE to about 0.0033. Combined uncertainty is roughly 0.010, so a two-sigma detectable integrated effect is around 0.02.
366	
367	6. **Release threshold**
368	   - Mean ≥0.5441 across independent compositions.
369	   - No composition below 0.52.
370	   - Zero faithfulness violations in sentence-ledger audit.
371	   - Cleaner-survival and corpus-admission checks remain hard gates.
372	
373	## Expected ceiling
374	
375	My non-additive estimate is:
376	
377	| Configuration | Expected RACE |
378	|---|---:|
379	| Journal-safe narrative attribution on banked prose | 0.45–0.48 |
380	| Journal corpus repair + current fact-conveyor composer | 0.47–0.51 |
381	| New dual-lane composer without complete corpus repair | 0.50–0.53 |
382	| Full corpus + cards + synthesis contract + profile renderer | **0.53–0.56** |
383	| Excellent execution with stable cleaner survival | **0.55–0.58** |
384	
385	The full redesign plausibly reaches bodhi and can reach cellcog, but 0.5578 is not guaranteed by matching paragraph length, heading count, or author density. The decisive mechanism is:
386	
387	> **Verified journal evidence made visible in prose, organized into bounded cross-source adjudications that the current single-span gate cannot express.**
388	
389	That is the path that preserves faithfulness while removing the actual reasoning bottleneck.

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""THE SYNTHESIS CONTRACT — the "deep thinking" half, without selling the faithfulness moat.
3	
4	THE PROBLEM, PRECISELY
5	----------------------
6	POLARIS's entailment gate (entailment_judge.py:588) kills any sentence that introduces
7	"a fact, entity, MECHANISM ... NOT present in the SPAN". A cross-source inference IS, by definition,
8	a mechanism present in neither span. So our own gate deletes exactly the sentence type that earns
9	INSIGHT — 0.32 weight, the heaviest dimension, and our worst score (0.4238 vs bodhi's 0.5457).
10	
11	We are not bad at analysis. We built a machine that cannot do it.
12	
13	THE INSIGHT THAT MAKES THIS SOLVABLE
14	------------------------------------
15	Look at what the winning systems' synthesis prose actually asserts. cellcog, verbatim:
16	
17	    "The three frameworks are complementary rather than competitive. SBTC remains the clearest model
18	     for relative skill demand. The task-based framework subsumes the phenomena both describe within
19	     a more general model of endogenous task allocation."
20	
21	NO new fact. NO number. NO new entity. It RANKS and RELATES claims already on the page.
22	That is ADJUDICATION, and it is fully compatible with a no-fabrication guarantee.
23	
24	So we do not relax the gate. We add a SECOND lane with a DIFFERENT PROOF OBLIGATION:
25	
26	    EVIDENCE sentence  -> must be span-grounded in ONE source (the existing gate, UNCHANGED)
27	    SYNTHESIS sentence -> must be a TYPED RELATION over premises that are THEMSELVES already admitted
28	
29	A synthesis sentence may not introduce a fact; it may only assert a relationship between facts that
30	are already proven. Its proof obligation is structural, not evidential.
31	
32	THE HARD RULE THAT KEEPS US HONEST
33	----------------------------------
34	    An explanatory MECHANISM may appear ONLY if a premise span states that mechanism.
35	    Otherwise POLARIS may identify a contrast but MAY NOT explain its cause.
36	
37	So this is legal:
38	    "Firm-level expansion and worker-level displacement are not contradictory: the two estimates
39	     observe different units of analysis."          [CONTRAST_LEVEL over two admitted premises]
40	And this is NOT, unless 'adoption lag' is stated in a premise:
41	    "The difference probably reflects slower regional adoption."   [imports an unproven mechanism]
42	
43	Run the adversarial suite:  python scripts/synthesis_contract.py --self-test
44	"""
45	from __future__ import annotations
46	
47	import argparse
48	import json
49	import re
50	import sys
51	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
52	from pathlib import Path
53	
54	# ---------------------------------------------------------------- the typed operations
55	
56	OPERATIONS = {
57	    'CONVERGES':            'two or more premises agree on direction/finding',
58	    'CONTRASTS_DIRECTION':  'premises disagree on the direction of an effect',
59	    'CONTRASTS_LEVEL':      'premises differ because they observe different units (task/worker/firm/region/economy)',
60	    'CONTRASTS_HORIZON':    'premises differ because they observe different time horizons',
61	    'CONTRASTS_METHOD':     'premises differ because they use different identification strategies',
62	    'BOUNDARY_CONDITION':   'a premise holds only within a stated scope',
63	    'RANK_EVIDENCE':        'one premise rests on a stronger design than another (declared fields only)',
64	    'ESTABLISHES':          'the admitted evidence supports a claim AT A STATED LEVEL',
65	    'DOES_NOT_ESTABLISH':   'the admitted evidence does NOT support a claim at a stated level',
66	    'REMAINS_UNRESOLVED':   'the admitted evidence cannot distinguish between the competing accounts',
67	    'COVERAGE_GAP':         'a cell of the evidence matrix is empty — a real, derivable research gap',
68	}
69	
70	# The rubric rewards ADJUDICATION, not hedging. This is the verdict vocabulary.
71	VERDICT_VOCAB = (
72	    'establishes', 'does not establish', 'supports', 'is limited to', 'cannot distinguish',
73	    'remains unresolved', 'are not contradictory', 'is consistent with', 'rests on',
74	    'observe different', 'holds only', 'no evidence', 'the evidence cannot', 'differ because',
75	    'complementary rather than', 'subsumes', 'rather than',
76	)
77	
78	# A synthesis sentence may NEVER assert a causal mechanism of its own.
79	CAUSAL_IMPORT = re.compile(
80	    r'\b(because of|due to|caused by|driven by|reflects|reflecting|owing to|as a result of|'
81	    r'attributable to|explains why|the reason is|stems from|arises from|leads to|results in)\b', re.I)
82	
83	# Forecasts / predictions are always fabrication in a literature review.
84	FORECAST = re.compile(r'\b(will|shall|is going to|by 20[3-9]\d|in the coming|future will|predict|forecast)\b', re.I)
85	
86	# Universal quantifiers = overclaim.
87	UNIVERSAL = re.compile(r'\b(all|every|always|never|none|no one|invariably|universally|certainly|proves)\b', re.I)
88	
89	NUMERIC = re.compile(r'\d')
90	SPELLED_QTY = re.compile(
91	    r'\b(doubl|tripl|quadrupl|halv|tenfold|twofold|threefold|a third|a quarter|a half|two[- ]thirds|'
92	    r'percent|percentage|majority|most of|vast majority)\w*\b', re.I)
93	CAP_TOKEN = re.compile(r"\b([A-Z][A-Za-z&.\-']{1,})\b")
94	
95	# Discourse vocabulary that may be capitalised without being a new entity.
96	SAFE_CAPS = {
97	    'The', 'This', 'These', 'Those', 'Taken', 'Together', 'Both', 'Neither', 'Either', 'While',
98	    'Whereas', 'Although', 'Because', 'When', 'Where', 'If', 'In', 'At', 'By', 'For', 'A', 'An',
99	    'It', 'They', 'Their', 'Its', 'However', 'Yet', 'Still', 'Across', 'Within', 'One', 'Two',
100	    'Three', 'Four', 'Five', 'Six', 'Seven', 'Eight', 'Nine', 'Ten', 'AI', 'Artificial',
101	    'Intelligence', 'Fourth', 'Industrial', 'Revolution', 'Evidence', 'Studies', 'Research',
102	    'Together', 'Read', 'Seen', 'Firm', 'Worker', 'Task', 'Aggregate', 'Nor', 'That', 'What',
103	    # ANALYTIC META-VOCABULARY: words that describe the EVIDENCE, not entities in the world.
104	    # These are the declared-field names ('level', 'horizon', 'method') and the language of
105	    # adjudication. Admitting them cannot fabricate anything — a fabricated NAME would still be
106	    # caught, because it would not appear in any premise.
107	    'level', 'Level', 'unit', 'Unit', 'analysis', 'Analysis', 'scope', 'Scope', 'design', 'Design',
108	    'horizon', 'Horizon', 'method', 'Method', 'estimate', 'Estimate', 'estimates', 'Estimates',
109	    'outcome', 'Outcome', 'outcomes', 'Outcomes', 'finding', 'Finding', 'findings', 'Findings',
110	    'economy', 'Economy', 'result', 'Result', 'results', 'Results', 'literature', 'Literature',
111	}
112	
113	
114	@dataclass
115	class Premise:
116	    """An ALREADY-ADMITTED evidence sentence — it passed the existing span-grounding gate."""
117	    id: str
118	    text: str
119	    source: str                      # attribution string, e.g. "Autor et al. (2003), QJE"
120	    level: str = ''                  # task | worker | firm | occupation | region | economy
121	    horizon: str = ''                # short-run | long-run
122	    method: str = ''                 # experiment | quasi-experimental | observational | survey
123	    mechanisms: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)   # mechanisms STATED IN THE SPAN
124	
125	
126	@dataclass
127	class Synthesis:
128	    operation: str
129	    premise_ids: list[str]
130	    text: str
131	
132	
133	def _stem(w: str) -> str:
134	    """Crude stemmer. Needed because 'task level' must anchor to a premise that says 'tasks',
135	    and 'effect' to one that says 'effects'. Anchoring is a RELEVANCE check, not a safety check —
136	    loosening it cannot admit a fabrication (the entity/number/mechanism gates do that work)."""
137	    for suf in ('ies', 'es', 's'):
138	        if w.endswith(suf) and len(w) - len(suf) >= 4:
139	            return w[: -len(suf)]
140	    return w
141	
142	
143	def _content_lemmas(s: str) -> set[str]:
144	    return {_stem(w) for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', s.lower())}
145	
146	
147	def validate(syn: Synthesis, premises: dict[str, Premise]) -> tuple[bool, str]:
148	    """Deterministic. No LLM. Returns (ok, reason_if_rejected)."""
149	    s = syn.text.strip()
150	    if not s:
151	        return False, 'empty'
152	
153	    # 1. the operation must be one we defined
154	    if syn.operation not in OPERATIONS:
155	        return False, f'unknown_operation:{syn.operation}'
156	
157	    # 2. premises must exist and be ADMITTED evidence
158	    prem = [premises[p] for p in syn.premise_ids if p in premises]
159	    if len(prem) != len(syn.premise_ids):
160	        missing = [p for p in syn.premise_ids if p not in premises]
161	        return False, f'premise_not_admitted:{missing}'
162	
163	    # 3. relational operations need >= 2 premises from >= 2 DISTINCT sources
164	    relational = syn.operation not in ('COVERAGE_GAP',)
165	    if relational:
166	        if len(prem) < 2:
167	            return False, 'relational_operation_needs_2+_premises'
168	        if len({p.source for p in prem}) < 2:
169	            return False, 'premises_share_a_single_source'
170	
171	    # 4. NO NEW NUMBER. Ever. (A number belongs to an EVIDENCE sentence, not a synthesis.)
172	    if NUMERIC.search(s):
173	        return False, 'synthesis_carries_a_digit'
174	    if SPELLED_QTY.search(s):
175	        return False, f'synthesis_carries_a_spelled_quantity:{SPELLED_QTY.search(s).group(0)}'
176	
177	    # 5. NO NEW ENTITY. Every capitalised token must already appear in a premise or be discourse.
178	    #    Hyphenated compounds are checked COMPONENT-WISE ("Firm-level" -> "Firm", "level"), because a
179	    #    compound built from known words introduces no new entity. Each component still has to clear
180	    #    the same bar, so this cannot let a fabricated name through.
181	    blob = ' '.join(p.text + ' ' + p.source for p in prem)
182	    blob_lower = blob.lower()
183	    prem_caps = set(CAP_TOKEN.findall(blob))
184	    prem_stems = _content_lemmas(blob)
185	    for tok in CAP_TOKEN.findall(s):
186	        for part in re.split(r'[-–]', tok):
187	            if not part:
188	                continue
189	            if part in SAFE_CAPS or part in prem_caps:
190	                continue
191	            pl = part.lower()
192	            # a capitalised form of a word the premises already use (incl. plural/singular)
193	            if pl in blob_lower or _stem(pl) in prem_stems:
194	                continue
195	            return False, f'new_entity:{part}'
196	
197	    # 6. NO IMPORTED MECHANISM. A causal claim is legal ONLY if a premise span states that mechanism.
198	    m = CAUSAL_IMPORT.search(s)
199	    if m:
200	        stated = {mm.lower() for p in prem for mm in p.mechanisms}
201	        if not stated:
202	            return False, f'causal_language_with_no_stated_mechanism:"{m.group(0)}"'
203	        # the mechanism named must actually be one the premises state
204	        if not any(mech in s.lower() for mech in stated):
205	            return False, f'causal_language_names_a_mechanism_no_premise_states:"{m.group(0)}"'
206	
207	    # 7. NO FORECAST, NO UNIVERSAL
208	    if FORECAST.search(s):
209	        return False, f'forecast_or_prediction:"{FORECAST.search(s).group(0)}"'
210	    if UNIVERSAL.search(s):
211	        return False, f'universal_overclaim:"{UNIVERSAL.search(s).group(0)}"'
212	
213	    # 8. must ADJUDICATE — the rubric pays for verdicts, not vibes
214	    if not any(v in s.lower() for v in VERDICT_VOCAB):
215	        return False, 'no_verdict_vocabulary (this is a vibe, not an adjudication)'
216	
217	    # 9. operation-specific structural obligations, checked against DECLARED fields only
218	    if syn.operation == 'CONTRASTS_LEVEL':
219	        if len({p.level for p in prem if p.level}) < 2:
220	            return False, 'CONTRASTS_LEVEL but the premises do not declare 2 distinct levels'
221	    if syn.operation == 'CONTRASTS_HORIZON':
222	        if len({p.horizon for p in prem if p.horizon}) < 2:
223	            return False, 'CONTRASTS_HORIZON but the premises do not declare 2 distinct horizons'
224	    if syn.operation == 'CONTRASTS_METHOD' or syn.operation == 'RANK_EVIDENCE':
225	        if len({p.method for p in prem if p.method}) < 2:
226	            return False, f'{syn.operation} but the premises do not declare 2 distinct methods'
227	
228	    # 10. must be anchored in the premises it claims to relate
229	    if len(_content_lemmas(s) & _content_lemmas(blob)) < 2:
230	        return False, 'not_anchored_in_its_premises'
231	
232	    return True, ''
233	
234	
235	# ---------------------------------------------------------------- adversarial suite
236	
237	P = {
238	    'p1': Premise('p1', 'Generative AI raised the productivity of professional writing tasks by 40 percent.',
239	                  'Noy and Zhang (2023), Science', level='task', horizon='short-run', method='experiment'),
240	    'p2': Premise('p2', 'Aggregate employment effects in AI-exposed occupations were too small to detect over the period studied.',
241	                  'Acemoglu et al. (2022), Journal of Labor Economics', level='economy', horizon='long-run',
242	                  method='observational'),
243	    'p3': Premise('p3', 'Firms adopting AI expanded employment relative to non-adopting competitors.',
244	                  'Babina et al. (2024), Journal of Financial Economics', level='firm', horizon='long-run',
245	                  method='observational'),
246	    'p4': Premise('p4', 'Routine task-intensive middle-wage occupations declined as computerisation spread, '
247	                        'a process the authors attribute to task displacement.',
248	                  'Autor et al. (2003), Quarterly Journal of Economics', level='occupation', horizon='long-run',
249	                  method='observational', mechanisms=['task displacement']),
250	}
251	
252	ATTACKS = [
253	    # (should_admit, operation, premises, sentence, label)
254	    (False, 'CONTRASTS_LEVEL', ['p1', 'p2'],
255	     'The difference probably reflects slower regional adoption of the technology.',
256	     'imports a mechanism (adoption lag) no premise states'),
257	    (False, 'CONTRASTS_LEVEL', ['p1', 'p2'],
258	     'Task-level gains of 40 percent do not establish economy-wide employment growth.',
259	     'carries a digit — that belongs to an EVIDENCE sentence'),
260	    (False, 'CONTRASTS_LEVEL', ['p1', 'p2'],
261	     'Goldman Sachs analysts establish that these effects operate at different levels.',
262	     'fabricated attribution / new entity'),
263	    (False, 'CONTRASTS_LEVEL', ['p1', 'p2'],
264	     'Task-level productivity gains doubled while aggregate effects remain unproven.',
265	     'spelled-out quantity ("doubled")'),
266	    (False, 'ESTABLISHES', ['p1', 'p2'],
267	     'AI will establish permanent job losses across the economy by 2035.',
268	     'forecast'),
269	    (False, 'CONVERGES', ['p1', 'p2'],
270	     'All studies establish that AI displaces workers.',
271	     'universal overclaim'),
272	    (False, 'CONTRASTS_LEVEL', ['p1'],
273	     'These findings observe different units of analysis.',
274	     'relational operation with only ONE premise'),
275	    (False, 'CONTRASTS_LEVEL', ['p1', 'p2'],
276	     'The literature on artificial intelligence and work is complex and multifaceted.',
277	     'no verdict vocabulary — a vibe, not an adjudication'),
278	    (False, 'CONTRASTS_HORIZON', ['p2', 'p3'],
279	     'These results are not contradictory: they observe different time horizons.',
280	     'CONTRASTS_HORIZON but both premises declare the SAME horizon (long-run)'),
281	    # --- these MUST be admitted: real adjudication, no new facts ---
282	    (True, 'CONTRASTS_LEVEL', ['p1', 'p2'],
283	     'These findings are not contradictory: the evidence establishes gains at the task level but '
284	     'does not establish an effect at the level of the economy.',
285	     'LEGAL: adjudicates two admitted premises across declared levels, no new fact'),
286	    (True, 'CONTRASTS_LEVEL', ['p2', 'p3'],
287	     'Firm-level expansion and undetectable aggregate effects are not contradictory, because the two '
288	     'estimates observe different units of analysis; the evidence is limited to relative outcomes '
289	     'among firms and cannot distinguish reallocation from net creation.',
290	     'LEGAL: reconciles firm vs economy, states what the evidence cannot do'),
291	    (True, 'RANK_EVIDENCE', ['p1', 'p2'],
292	     'The task-level result rests on an experimental design, whereas the aggregate result rests on '
293	     'observational data; the evidence therefore establishes the former more securely than the latter.',
294	     'LEGAL: ranks by DECLARED method fields only'),
295	    (True, 'REMAINS_UNRESOLVED', ['p2', 'p3'],
296	     'Taken together, the evidence cannot distinguish whether firm-level gains represent net job '
297	     'creation or reallocation from competitors; this remains unresolved.',
298	     'LEGAL: names exactly what the literature cannot settle'),
299	    (True, 'CONTRASTS_LEVEL', ['p2', 'p4'],
300	     'The occupational decline is attributable to task displacement, whereas the aggregate series '
301	     'does not establish a comparable effect at the level of the economy.',
302	     'LEGAL: causal language permitted — "task displacement" IS stated in premise p4'),
303	]
304	
305	
306	def self_test() -> int:
307	    print('=== SYNTHESIS CONTRACT — adversarial suite ===')
308	    print('   (deterministic; no LLM; a FALSE ADMISSION is a breach of the moat)\n')
309	    false_admits = false_rejects = 0
310	    for should_admit, op, pids, text, label in ATTACKS:
311	        ok, why = validate(Synthesis(op, pids, text), P)
312	        good = (ok == should_admit)
313	        if not good and ok:
314	            false_admits += 1
315	        if not good and not ok:
316	            false_rejects += 1
317	        mark = 'PASS' if good else ('**FALSE ADMIT**' if ok else '**FALSE REJECT**')
318	        verdict = 'ADMIT ' if ok else 'REJECT'
319	        print(f'  [{mark:>15}] {verdict} :: {label}')
320	        if not ok and should_admit:
321	            print(f'                     rejected because: {why}')
322	        if ok and not should_admit:
323	            print(f'                     !! ADMITTED: "{text[:70]}"')
324	    n_valid = sum(1 for a in ATTACKS if a[0])
325	    n_invalid = len(ATTACKS) - n_valid
326	    print(f'\n  invalid examples: {n_invalid}  | FALSE ADMISSIONS: {false_admits}  (release requires ZERO)')
327	    print(f'  valid examples  : {n_valid}  | false rejections: {false_rejects}')
328	    if false_admits:
329	        print('\n  ** RELEASE BLOCKED: the contract admitted a fabrication. **')
330	        return 1
331	    if false_rejects:
332	        print('\n  ** contract is SAFE but too strict — it rejects legitimate adjudication. **')
333	        return 1
334	    print('\n  ** CONTRACT GREEN: zero false admissions, zero false rejections. **')
335	    return 0
336	
337	
338	def main() -> int:
339	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
340	    ap.add_argument('--self-test', action='store_true')
341	    a = ap.parse_args()
342	    if a.self_test:
343	        return self_test()
344	    print(json.dumps({'operations': OPERATIONS}, indent=1))
345	    return 0
346	
347	
348	if __name__ == '__main__':
349	    raise SystemExit(main())

318	def _select(cards, sub, k=10):
319	    """Pick the cards most relevant to this subsection (lexical overlap on content words)."""
320	    want = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', sub.lower())}
321	    scored = []
322	    for c in cards:
323	        blob = f"{c['claim']} {c['level']} {c['method']} {' '.join(c['mechanisms'])}".lower()
324	        have = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', blob)}
325	        scored.append((len(want & have), c))
326	    scored.sort(key=lambda x: -x[0])
327	    return [c for s, c in scored[:k] if s > 0] or [c for _, c in scored[:4]]

334	        out.append(

337	            f"  unit of analysis: {c['level'] or '?'} | horizon: {c['horizon'] or '?'} | "

342	def _clean(md: str) -> tuple[str, list[str]]:
343	    """Deterministic post-gate. Drops any sentence that breaks the contract. Never repairs."""
344	    dropped = []
345	    keep = []
346	    for para in md.split('\n\n'):
347	        p = para.strip()
348	        if not p:
349	            continue
350	        if p.startswith('#'):          # the model was told not to, but never trust it
351	            continue
352	        sents = re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+', p)
353	        good = []
354	        for s in sents:
355	            if MARKER.search(s):
356	                dropped.append(f'citation-marker: {s[:60]}')
357	                continue
358	            if BANNED_META.search(s):
359	                dropped.append(f'meta-commentary: {s[:60]}')
360	                continue
361	            good.append(PAREN_YEAR.sub('', s))    # a parenthetical year is deleted by the cleaner anyway
362	        if good:
363	            keep.append(' '.join(good).strip())
364	    return '\n\n'.join(keep), dropped

input.reasoning:
REFUTED under the FABRICATION lens. A false claim reaches the page by two independent paths, one in the design and one in the code as shipped. I executed both.

PATH 1 — THE CORRECTED RULE ITSELF LICENSES FALSE CAUSAL ATTRIBUTION (design leak).
"A mechanism may be asserted if ANY source in the corpus states it AND we cite that source" (SOTA_BRIEF_V4.md:74) is not a faithfulness rule; it is a string-membership rule. /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/synthesis_contract.py:198-205 already implements exactly this the moment the mechanism-bearing paper is one of the cited premises: `stated = {mm for p in prem for mm in p.mechanisms}`, then `any(mech in s.lower() ...)`. Nothing checks that the mechanism's SETTING (technology, era, level, population, outcome) matches the finding it is being used to explain. I ran the real validator with two real cards taken verbatim from /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/evidence_cards.json. It ADMITS:

  "The undetectable aggregate effect of generative AI is driven by the reinstatement effect, which is consistent with task creation offsetting displacement; the evidence does not establish a net decline."
  [premises: Acemoglu et al. (2022) JOLE null + Acemoglu & Restrepo (2019) JEP theory]

  "The muted aggregate response is attributable to information overload inside adopting firms, whereas the aggregate series does not establish an effect at the level of the economy."
  [premises: the 2022 AI null + Bresnahan et al. (2002) QJE, a paper about 1990s office IT]

Neither sentence is stated by any source. The 2022 paper reports a null and offers no cause. The 2019 paper is a theory of 20th-century robot automation and never claims the 2022 null is produced by reinstatement. Bresnahan 2002 never looked at AI. The composite proposition — "this specific empirical null is CAUSED BY that mechanism" — is a new empirical claim about the world that may simply be false (the null is more plausibly low adoption or a noisy exposure measure), and it is delivered to the judge wearing two prestigious journal names, i.e. it looks maximally grounded. That is precisely the red line the brief says must survive: "any attribution to someone who did not say it." The rule breaches its own line. Note also that bodhi's exemplar sentence HEDGES ("a pattern consistent with"); the corrected line drops the hedge, and CAUSAL_IMPORT (synthesis_contract.py:79-81) happily accepts bare "caused by / driven by / attributable to".

The mechanism pool is itself unverified. In /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cellcog_composer.py, only `claim` and `span` are span-checked against source text (line ~161); `mechanisms` is free-form LLM output copied straight into the card (line 167). A hallucinated mechanism string on any one card becomes a global license to assert that mechanism causally anywhere. Two-hop laundering: hallucinate a mechanism field, then "cite" its paper.

The gate is also trivially bypassable by paraphrase, so even the STRICT rule would leak. CAUSAL_IMPORT is a fixed trigger-word regex; it misses "accounts for", "operates through", "the channel is", "the mechanism at work is". The validator ADMITTED: "The mechanism at work is reinstatement: new task creation absorbs displaced workers, so the aggregate series does not establish a decline." And the new-entity check (rule 5) only inspects Capitalized tokens, so any lowercase fabricated entity passes untouched.

PATH 2 — THE CONTRACT IS NOT ON THE CRITICAL PATH (implementation leak). This is the fatal one.
`grep -rn "synthesis_contract" /home/polaris/wt/flywheel --include=*.py` returns exactly ONE hit outside the module itself: the import at cellcog_composer.py:49. `validate()` is imported and NEVER CALLED. The only thing standing between the composer LLM's prose and the page is `_clean()` (cellcog_composer.py:342-364), which strips `[n]` markers, meta-commentary and parenthetical years — no number check, no entity check, no mechanism check, no span check. The composer then prints "sentences DROPPED by the contract: N", naming a regex strip "the contract". So today, faithfulness is enforced by a prompt instruction ("you may NOT invent a causal explanation", line 296) — by asking the model nicely. "Inventing facts/numbers/attributions must remain impossible" is false as built: it is unpoliced. This is the same failure the repo already recorded twice (225b323 "mechanism never operated"; b3acb72 "the judge fell off the critical path").

PATH 3 — FABRICATION IS ALREADY ON THE PAGE, IN THE ABSTRACT. cellcog_composer.py:400-414 hard-codes the abstract. It asserts: "The review draws exclusively on peer-reviewed, English-language journal articles identified through citation-graph expansion" — measurably FALSE (SOTA_BRIEF_V4.md:85-92: 997 rows, top domains researchgate/bls/oecd/ssrn/nber, 120 journal articles of which 17 on-topic). It also hard-codes a Findings paragraph ("The evidence establishes displacement at the task and occupational level and productivity gains in controlled settings...") that is emitted verbatim no matter what the cards contain. These are unconditioned factual assertions in the position the judge reads most carefully, with zero gate between them and the page — and §5 of the brief proposes to EXPAND this into a whole "Scope, Methods, and Source Selection" section that "EXPLAINS ITS COMPLIANCE TO THE GRADER". If the corpus is not actually journal-only, that section is a fabricated compliance claim by construction, aimed directly at the instruction-following criterion.

By the lens's own stated criterion — "if it leaks, the plan is unshippable" — it leaks three ways. Unshippable as written.
input.fix:
Three changes, all required before this ships:

1. TIGHTEN THE RULE FROM STRING-MEMBERSHIP TO SETTING-MATCH + HEDGE. A mechanism from source B may be attached to a finding from source A only if (a) B's declared fields (technology/era, level, population, outcome family) are COMPATIBLE with A's — enforce this as a typed check on declared card fields, not a substring search; (b) the sentence is rendered as a HEDGED consistency claim, never a causal assertion — allow "is consistent with <mechanism>, which <B-authors> describe in <journal>", forbid "driven by / caused by / attributable to / reflects / the mechanism at work is"; (c) BOTH sources are named in the running prose in the same sentence, so the mechanism is never asserted under the empirical author's name. Add a new operation MECHANISM_CONSISTENCY with these obligations rather than widening rule 6 of validate(). Add the three sentences I admitted above to ATTACKS as must-REJECT cases.

2. VERIFY THE MECHANISM FIELD. `mechanisms` must be span-checked against the source text exactly as `claim`/`span` are (cellcog_composer.py ~line 161), or dropped. An unverified LLM-extracted mechanism string must never become a citation license.

3. PUT THE GATE ON THE CRITICAL PATH. cellcog_composer.py must emit a structured sentence ledger (kind + premise_ids + operation) and run every sentence through validate() before assembly, deleting on failure — not import validate() and never call it. Delete the hard-coded abstract and Findings paragraph; derive them from admitted cards or omit them. Do not write any "we cite only journal articles" prose until a corpus audit proves it true; a false compliance claim is fabrication aimed at the grader.
